data.title: Fitt's Law
data.description: Adobe's interfaces are designed so that frequent and desired actions are larger and easier to reach.
data.technical_spec: Clickable buttons, responsive design, touch-friendly elements

data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: The complexity of menus and choices is minimized to speed up decision-making.
data.technical_spec: Simplified navigation, categorized options, dropdown menus

data.title: Gestalt Principles
data.description: The site uses visual hierarchy and grouping to assist user perception.
data.technical_spec: Consistency in design patterns, alignment, use of whitespace

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Conformity to established user expectations in design and navigation flows.
data.technical_spec: Conventional layouts, familiar icons, consistent user interface

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: An aesthetically pleasing design enhances perceived usability.
data.technical_spec: Clean design, high-quality graphics, brand-aligned color palette
